response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a broadly skilled full-stack and infrastructure engineer with a strong TDD background and genuine distributed systems exposure, but he fails to meet the most critical minimum qualification by a wide margin: the role requires 8 years of C++ experience, and his resume shows only roughly 6 months of professional C++ work at National Instruments plus a bachelor thesis project. C++ does not even appear in his skills section. Beyond that disqualifying gap, he has no demonstrated cloud compute platform ownership, no formal technical leadership of cloud or DevOps teams, and his infrastructure experience—while real—is oriented around web micro-services and Kubernetes deployment rather than compute, storage, or hardware architecture.
strengths: Genuine distributed systems experience: low-latency micro-services at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second demonstrates credible large-scale systems work
Strong software craftsmanship and testing culture: TDD advocacy, workshops, open-source test tooling, and CI/CD practice across multiple roles signals high code quality standards
Broad infrastructure exposure: Kubernetes, AWS CDK, Docker, GitLab CI, and DevOps tooling show operational awareness beyond pure development
concerns: C++ experience is approximately 6 months of professional work against the 8-year minimum requirement—this is a disqualifying gap for the role as written
No cloud compute platform leadership: career is oriented around product/web engineering and individual contribution, with no evidence of owning a platform serving downstream engineering teams
Formal technical leadership is absent: staff-level titles appear but mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers in a lead capacity is not demonstrated
